WET HOT AMERICAN SUMMER
written by Michael Showalter & David Wain
Table Read Revisions by Jeff
Movie accurate, not cast yet

February 23, 2026
Second Draft

“Jane” by Jefferson Starship begins with opening TITLES over
black”

CUT TO:
EXT. CAMPFIRE SITE - NIGHT
Music KICKS IN.

A large group of summer camp counselors hanging out by a
campfire, their faces lit only by the flames. The counselors
dance and play guitar to the music, snuggle under blanket make
marshmallow s’mores, feel each other up, play fight, make out.
TITLE: AUGUST 18, 1981
MUSIC / TITLES CONTINUE
EXT. VARIOUS - DAWN

The sun rises on a serene lake in rural New England to reveal
Camp Firewood: A dozen wooden cabins, basketball court, ball
field, rec hall, flagpole and mess hall. The last image is of a
GROUNDSKEEPER who peels and eats a banana, the sound of which
dissolves with...
INT. KATIE’S BUNK - DAWN

The banana-like sound of ravenous adolescent MAKING OUT
emanates from each bunk bed in the cabin.

At one point, a 14 year old BUNK BOY emerges from under one of
the blankets, squinting from the light coming in.

BUNK BOY
(in a loud whisper)
You guys!
From under each blanket, a boy’s tousled head emerges.

BUNK BOY (cont’d)
We gotta get back!

EIGHT BOYS now come out from under the blankets, separating
from the EIGHT GIRLS they’ve been making out with. The boys are
fully dressed in dark sweatsuits.
They flip up their goods and DASH out of the bunk.
EXT. KATIE’S BUNK - DAWN
Music and credits continue.

They exchange rushed “hello’s” as they pass a group of GIRLS,
also in sweatsuits, trekking back to their bunks in the
opposite direction.

The boys break onto the main field. Dawn. Hundreds of sweat
suited campers scurry about.

Everyone -- campers, counselors and staff -- are awake,
desperately trying to make it back before the day begins.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BETH’S OFFICE/EXT. PROFESSOR’S COTTAGE - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
BETH, the camp director, wearing her pajamas, sits on the stoop
of the camp office, sipping coffee with the NURSE.  Boys and
girls clumsily sneak by.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BETH’S OFFICE/EXT. PROFESSOR’S COTTAGE - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
BETH
(halfheartedly to the kids)
You guys aren’t supposed to be out
of your bunks.  You’re in
trouble...
INT. COOP’S BUNK - DAWN

COOP, a 17 year old counselor, sleeps in his cot. He is
surrounded by BOBBY, TIMOTHY and several 10-year-old boys in
their underwear.

CAMPERS
(singing softly)
”Standing in the rain, with his
head hung low”

COOP
(eyes opening)
Guys, please don’t sing. I’m up.
I’m up.

CAMPERS
(a little louder)
”Couldn’t get a ticket, it was a
sold out show. Heard the roar of
the crowd he could picture the
scene. Put his ear to the wall
then like a distant scream. He
heard one guitar just blew him
away”

The campers SCREAM, jump on top of Coop, banging their feet
and air-guitaring with badminton rackets and whiffle-ball
bats.

COOP
Okay, okay!
The boys run off, giggling.

Coop puts on a ratty sweatsuit and splashes some Polo cologne
on his chin.
INT. KATIE’S BUNK - DAWN

The eight girls, now awake in their ratty sweatsuits, are
crowded around a small mirror, putting on makeup.
Their counselor, KATIE, 16, is standing by the door.

KATIE
Let’s go girls, you look hot. The
Bunk 5 boys are going to freak out
when they see you at kick ball.
Let’s go!
EXT. BETH’S OFFICE/EXT. PROFESSOR’S COTTAGE

In the distance, Beth sees PROFESSOR NEUMANN, 40’s working in
the garden outside of his cottage across the lake. They
awkwardly smile at each other.
EXT. COOP’S BUNK - DAWN

As Coop leaves his bunk, we hear the voice-over of ARTY, the
DJ of the camp radio station. Under this voice-over, we follow
Coop as he heads out to the field, among everyone else.

ARTY (V.O.)
Good morning everybody, well, it’s
the last day of camp. It’ll be sad
to shut down WCFW for the summer.
But not to worry, because those of
you in the Bethesda, Chevy Chase
area can hear me all winter long
on Jewish Day School Radio 89.9 FM
- The Fox! For those select few of
you who went all summer, all eight
weeks, without finding that
special someone, today’s your day.
You don’t want to go back home and
lie to your friends about a summer
romance that didn’t happen. You
don’t want to be the one person
who doesn’t have any one to kiss
tonight after the talent show.
3.

ARTY (V.O.)
So seize the day, Camp Firewood.
It’s your last!

Next to Paul is MOOSE, 10, an overweight crew-cutted spectacle

The whole camp is assembled in front of the flag pole.
Everybody is straining to stay awake. Moose silently raises the
flag up the pole.

paragraphs.text: Coop stands with his campers keeping them in line.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
Katie, after-glowing, stands with her campers next to Coop.
Coop steals a glance at her. She smiles at him. He turns away,
pretending like he was looking everywhere.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
Coop, now extremely self-conscious, shifts awkwardly trying to
look cool.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

(joking seductively)
Coop, do you like watching me and
Andy make-out?

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

No! What do you mean? That’s a
stupid thing to say...

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text: Katie makes sexy faces at Coop. He is totally embarrassed.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
KATIE
Oh God, Coop...you look so sexy in
that sweatshirt.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

Uh huh.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

Oooh, your ass is so hot.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

Stop it.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

Do you wanna sit on my face?

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
COOP
Okay! I get it. It’s very funny.
Now stop.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
KATIE
(hugging him)
OH come on, Coop, you know I’m
just kidding.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
COOP
I know and it’s very very funny.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
KATIE
Okay, I’m sorry. I’ll make it up
to you.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
COOP
That’s okay. You’ve done enough.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
KATIE
I’m gonna find you a woman today-

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
COOP
Honestly, don’t worry about it-

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
COOP
Well I guess I’ll cancel that
order of onions and limburger
cheese I made for lunch. Ha!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
KATIE
Yeah...

I want you inside me.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
Katie walks into the dining hall with all the kids. VICTOR, 17,

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
VICTOR
Coop, were you just talking to
Katie? What was that?

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

I don’t know, but it was bar none,
the longest conversation I’ve ever
had with her in six years at camp.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
VICTOR
So does she want to fuck me? Is
that was she was asking you about?
Because tell her that I will. In
fact don’t tell her that - tell
her I might.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

Victor, I don’t know quite how to
say this but your name didn’t come
up.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:

I know she wants to fuck me. Why
else would she talk to you. No
offense.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. FLAGPOLE (NEXT TO DINING HALL) - DAWN
paragraphs.text:
COOP
None taken.
EXT. PROFESSOR’S COTTAGE - MORNING
Beth approaches PROFESSOR NEUMANN as he tends to his garden.

BETH
Hi there, I’m Beth.

PROFESSOR
Ah, very nice to meet you. I’m
Henry.

BETH
I’ve seen you here all summer and
I thought I’d, you know,
“Introduce Myself”.

She hold out her hand to shake. Unaware of what she’s doing
Professor hands her a gardening trowel. Making the most of the
situation, Beth playfully waves the trowel in the air. The top
part of it flies off, leaving her with just the handle. They
giggle awkwardly.

BETH
So...

PROFESSOR
So...

Henry leans on a flower pot. His fist sinks into the dirt. He
pretends it didn’t happen and leaves his hand in the pot.

PROFESSOR (cont’d)
(trying to act normal)
So you work over at the camp, huh?

BETH
Yes, I’m the director. How about
you?

Beth gives Professor a flirtatious smile and sits down on the
grass. The Professor doesn’t sit.

PROFESSOR
Me? Nah - I don’t work over there.
Beth pops back up as if she never sat down.

BETH
No? I mean, I know you don’t work
at the camp, silly. I’m the
director.

PROFESSOR
Right. (pause) Right! I teach
Astrophysics over at the college
during the school year, and I. Are
her this summer to relax and
reflect.

BETH
So you’re a professor, huh?

PROFESSOR
Yeah, well, actually Associate
Professor.

BETH
Well what do you teach?
Professor nonchalantly runs his mulch hand through his hair.

PROFESSOR
Well, like I said before,
Astrophysics. I study pace, the
stars, comets, you know, things
like that.

BETH
Oooh - space! The “final frontier”
- right?

PROFESSOR
Right. Thanks for everything.

BETH
Hey - would you be willing to come
teach some of the campers about
space?

PROFESSOR
(sheepish)
Oh no, I couldn’t possibly...

BETH
Oh please, they’d love to hear
about the-

PROFESSOR
I SAID NO!!! I’m sorry.
Beth is embarrassed.

Victor catches Abby’s eye. She seductively licks her plastic

INT. MESS HALL - MORNING

Nearby, a 13 year old boy in an “I Hate Mondays” t-shirt and a
black cape gets out of his chair and goes over to a table of
girls. He addresses them, rolling a multi-sided die in his
hand.

CAPED BOY
Excuse me ladies. You may remember
me as the guy who came to dinner a
few weeks ago with underwear on my
head. My name is Keith Stack.
9.

CAPED BOY
I’m from Millburn, New Jersey,
state bird, the mosquito. This
completes my ninth full summer at
Firewood. And as you may have
heard, I’m a recently crowned
class B Dungeon Master, so if
anyones wants to play D&D today,
please speak now or forever hold
your peace. Anyone?
He gets no response. He looks to a cute young girl, ALEXA.

CAPED BOY
Alexa, maybe you’d like to join
in? We do need a druid. You’ve

spell on me.

ALEXA
In your dreams, douchebag.

CAPED BOY
Douchebags are hygienic products.
I take that as a compliment. Thank
you.
Caped Boy goes back to his table, beaming with pride.

ALEXA
Eeww. Ewww. Eewww!
INT. MESS HALL - MORNING

Beth sits at another table, chatting with older staff and

BETH
We’ve made it to the end of the
summer, and everyone’s still in
one piece -- except for a couple
of kids, who are lepers...

Good one, Beth.

Gene brings in a tin platter covered in overlapping fried eggs

Thank you Gene!

Without turning back around to face her, Gene gives Beth the

Beth looks up to see Professor Neumann shyly coming into the

PROFESSOR
Where would I find the -- how
should I put this -- the sci-fi,
nerdy...
JJ’s not sure exactly what he mans.

PROFESSOR
...the “indoor” kids.
Without hesitation, he points to Caped Boy’s table.

JJ
Bunk 3.
Professor goes over there.

PROFESSOR
Hey guys. I’m associate Professor
Neumann. Anyone want to spend the
last day of camp doing science
projects?

Five campers hands shoot into the air: CAPED BOY, MORK BOY.
CURE GIRL, and MALLRAT GIRL

Beth and Professor catch each other’s eyes across the room.
They smile.
INT. MESS HALL (KITCHEN) - MORNING
At the serving area, Gene and Gary:

GENE
I seen more fucked up shit happen
in five minutes out there than
you’ll see in your whole fuckin’
life!

GARY
Yeah well Gene - I’m really sorry.
If I could change history, I
would.

Fuck you! Now we need to make

Yeah --

-- In the pantry over the sink!
Right next to my bottle of dick

GARY
Did you say “dick cream”?

GENE
No! I said, uh, stick team! You

Forget about it. Go away. Leave me

GARY
I’m pretty sure you said “dick

INT. MESS HALL - MORNING

Back at Beth’s table, she leans her chair back to where Victor
is sitting.

BETH
Victor - I need you and Neil to
take the Eagles out on Moose River
today. I made them a promise.

VICTOR
This is my last day of camp, Beth.
That’s not gonna work for me.

BETH
Oh! That’s not gonna work for you!
How about this as a compromise:
The kids are gonna go out on the
Moose River. And you and Neil are
gonna take them.

VICTOR
What?!?

Just outside the mess hall, Katie is kissing ANDY, 17, a greasy

Andy leans into the cafeteria door and yells in.

Hey JJ - save me a waffle!

Andy come on! This is serious! I

Fuck you, dyke!

I’m gonna go finish my breakfast.
I love you baby. It’s so good

Andy kisses her and nonchalantly reenters the mess hall, high

See you in macrame!

It’s like I’m back at the
Cleveland Playhouse. Your craft is
a muscle, you need to exercise it.
Take a break and think about what
you’ve done.
Coop runs in.

COOP
Hey Susie - have you seen Katie
anywhere?

SUSIE
No why?

COOP
No particular reason. If you see
her will you tell her I was
looking for her?

SUSIE
Sure.
(to performers)
Alright, again, with passion.
EXT. KATIE’S BUNK -
Coop goes into Katie’s cabin.

COOP
Katie? Are you in here?
He sees Katie on the bed, in tears. Andy is there.

KATIE
Coop, I want to talk to you later.

COOP
Oh yeah, sure.
Andy flips him the bird. Coop leaves.
EXT. KATIE’S BUNK - DAY
Coop runs outside. Raises his arms in victory, all excited.
EXT. MESS HALL - DAY
ON THE HILL, Paul plays his guitar. Moose stands next to her.

MOOSE
(at the top of his lungs)
DINNER!!!!
INT. MESS HALL - DAY

Everyone’s eating dinner. McKinley and Ben sit at one of the
tables.

MCKINLEY
Where’re JJ and Gary?

BEN
Dunno.

MCKINLEY
I’m worried they might have found
out about us.
JJ and Gary apparent at the door.

GARY
Hey! McKinley and Ben!
McKinley and Ben worriedly look to the door.

JJ
This is for you!

JJ and Gary roll a huge box from “CRATE AND BARREL”, Into the
hall.

JJ (CONT’D)
It’s a chaise lounge. We didn’t
know if you guys maybe already had
it. We have the receipt if you do.

MCKINLEY
No! We don’t have it! Thank you!

BEN
It goes with the chenille throw
cloth Beth’s sister got us.

Everyone applauds as McKinley and Ben exchange hugs with Gary
and JJ.
INT. KITCHEN - DAY
Gene is cleaning up after dinner prep.

GENE
Cooking slop for the grunts in
‘Nam was easier than this.

Reveal Gene is addressing a TALKING CAN OF VEGETABLES whose lid
moves up and down when it talks.

CAN OF VEGETABLES
We had no place being over there.
It was a war that we couldn’t win.

GENE
Yeah, well I’m gonna go smear some
mud on my ass.

CAN OF VEGETABLES
What?!?

GENE
Huh, oh I said - “I hear my bud is
in class” I gotta call my bud and
see how his his, how his grades
are. I gotta go. Leave me alone.
Go away.

CAN OF VEGETABLES
Now hold on, Gene. I don’t care
what you do in your private time,
but don’t lie about it. You
clearly said “smear mud on my
ass.” And I’ll tell you something
- you wanna smear mud on your ass?
Smear mud on your ass, just be
honest about it. Look Gene, I’ve
never told anyone this before, but
I can suck my own dick, and I do
it, a lot. There, I said it. I was
honest. And you know what? Being
honest makes yo u feel better.
Gene nods his head pensively.
INT. RADIO STATION - DAY
Arty wears dark glasses and Viking hat.

ARTY
Arright, arright, cruising towards
happy hour. Normally at this point
I’d run down the list of after
work parties goin’ on, but summer
camp isn’t exactly the ideal demo
for that sort of programming.
Lookin’ towards our evening meal -
affectionately dubbed by those in
the know as “dinner”. Let’s talk
with Gary, our man in the kitchen,
about what’s cookin’ tonight. “The
Last Supper,” if you’ll indulge
the biblical reference. I know
Gary doesn’t mind!
Arty waits for Gary to respond.

ARTY (CONT’D)
So end the suspense for us, Gary.
What do we have on tap for dinner
tonight?
Beat. Arty enthusiastically motions for Gary to talk.

GARY
Spaghetti, salad, bread.

Arty hands Gary a ten dollar bill and mouths the words, “Thank
you”. Gary exits.

ARTY
Nice to see you, Gary. I’d love to
talk to you more but I gotta
little Lovin’ Spoonfull two-for
cookie’ on my stove. And don’t
forget to be at the field later to
cheer on Coop and the softball
team for the big game against Camp
Tiger Claw. Never a dull moment
here -- in the Beehive!

During this monologue we see the backside of Arty’s console.
There are no wires connecting it to anything.

“Summer in the City” by Lovin’ Spoonfull starts and continues
as we...
EXT. MESS HALL - DAY

Across the main lawn Coop sees Andy and Katie walking up from
Katie’s bunk towards the mess hall. She and Andy stop, talk for
a beat, then Andy walks away from her into the mess hall. Katie
lingers. Coop runs up to her.

COOP
Katie! I’m sorry about before. I
hope that wasn’t bad timing.
He tries to grab her hand. She doesn’t let him.

KATIE
Coop we have to talk.

COOP
Okay.

KATIE
I...I think that what happened
between us before was...was a
mistake.
Beat. Coop thinks, trying desperately to keep pace.

COOP
What?

KATIE
I wasn’t thinking straight. It was
wrong.
Coop lets this sing in.

COOP
It wasn’t wrong. Katie, I don’t -
why are you doing this?

KATIE
I’m really sorry Coop.

COOP
Tell me this is a joke. Please!

KATIE
I have a boyfriend, Coop. I
can’t...I can’t do this...I’m
sorry.
She walks toward the mess hall.

COOP
Wait! Katie!
He stops her by the door to the mess hall.

COOP (CONT’D)
When we first started hanging out
together this morning, we were
friends. But things changes. And
I’ve fallen in love with you. And
I know that if you’d just give me
a chance, I could make you feel so
good. I come to you not as your
buddy, not as your co-counselor,
but for the first time, as a Man.
A man who loves a woman. And wants
to hold her and provide for her,
(making a joke) and yes, have sex
with her, but no seriously... I
love the way you laugh. I love the
way your hair smells. I love it
that sometimes for no reason
you’re late for shul. And I don’t
care that you’re bow legged, and I
don’t care that you’re bilingual.
All I know is I would have said
“no” to every girl on your list,
because I’ve always wanted you.

KATIE
Coop, I don’t know what to say.
Coop turns to leave. Katie steps forward.

KATIE (CONT’D)
Coop wait!
Coop runs down the steps.
INT. MESS HALL - DAY
Gene pokes his head out the kitchen doors.

GENE
Beth, can I make a quick
announcement before we finish?

BETH
Sure.

He swings open the doors, completely wiping out Susie who is
carrying dirty dishes. The plates go flying and everyone looks
up toward him, but Gene doesn’t miss a beat

GENE
I want to thank all of you for a
terrific summer. Cooking for all
of you nice people has really
helped me get over the fact that I
fought in the Vietnam War. Have a
great winter, I’m going to go hump
the fridge!

BETH
What?
Beat. Gene takes a breath.

GENE
(like a military speech)
“Yes, folks. It’s true.
I said “I’m going to go hump the
fridge”. And what you may not
know, is that I own a bottle
of dick cream. I fondle my
sweaters, and I often like to
smear mud on my ass. Now
you’re probably asking yourselves,
isn’t he a weirdo? An outcast?
A loose cannon? Maybe. But I don’t
think so. I wanna introduce you
guys to someone.
He picks up the can of vegetables.

GENE (CONT’D)
This is my friend. I don’t know
who he is, but I do know this. In
a time when I was trying to hide
myself, from myself, he was there
to show me a new way, ‘cause I
couldn’t hide from him. And I can
be proud of who I am. I put it to
you, Camp Firewood, as we spend
our last dinner together: Be proud
of who you are. (Holding back
tears) Look at me Mom! I made it!
I’m okay!
The whole mess hall cheers and applauds Gene’s moving speech.

GENE (CONT’D)
And now, if you don’t mind I have
some unfinished business to attend
to.

Gary rolls out a large refrigerator. Gene wraps himself around
the fridge and thrusts his body against it. Gary, smiling at
Gene, rolls him and the fridge back into the kitchen. The crowd
cheers.
INT. MESS HALL - DAY

MOMENTS LATER: McKinley makes eye contact with JJ and Gary. JJ
mouths silently “One, two, three, go!”

McKinley, JJ, Gary and others jumps up and SEIZE Arty, assisted
from all sides.

Fighting huge resistance from Arty, they drag him out of the
mess hall and over to the showers. The whole camp follows and
helps.

ARTY (CONT’D)
Let me go! Let go of me! Let me
go! Quit it!
INT. SHOWERS - DAY
They push Arty into the showers, turn on the water full force.

ARTY
You fucking assholes! Why are you
doing this to me? I fucking hate
you!

McKinley and company emerge out of the showers with their arms
raised in victory as the crowd cheers them on.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text: Beth and Professor walk along the lake, hand in hand.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
I’m so happy now, Henry. I wonder
what will become of us tomorrow.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text: He’s just looking at the sky, mumbling to himself.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH (CONT’D)
You seem distracted. What’s the
matter?

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
Beth, tomorrow’s the least of our
problems.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Don’t tell me. You have crabs.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
No! Yes! But that’s not the point!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Then what is the point!?!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
This is the the time or the place
to get into it. Beth, meet me at
the picnic table in ten seconds,
and I’ll tell you all about it.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Okay.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
Professor walks out of frame. We follow Beth to the picnic
table, three yards away. The professor is waiting for her
there, now in his lab coat, surrounded by all of his students,
as well as telescopes and other equipment.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
Beth, hello. Well let’s get right
to it. You may remember that ten
seconds ago, I told you to meet me
here by the picnic table, so I
could tell you what’s going on.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Right.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
Earlier today, I noticed what I
thought was a planet or perhaps a
meteor in the evening sky, But I
now believe that object to be none
other than a renegade piece of
SkyLab, the NASA space station.
And it’s headed right for the
camp.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Oh my god.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
And not only that, there’s no way
of pinpointing exactly where it’s
going to land. It could kill us
all.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Jesus!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
Wait a minute...there might just
be a way to build a homemade
SkyLab tracking device!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Which accomplishes...

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
That way we could surmise the
location of impact. We could
prevent a catastrophe on the scale
of the Hindenburg. Beth, will you
help us?

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Anything you need.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
Let’s do it!
INT. ANDY’S BUNK - DAY

Coop goes inside to find Andy lying on his bed, the walls
plastered with photos of Christie Brinkley.

COOP
Andy. Can we talk for a second?

ANDY
Sure.

COOP
Well-

ANDY
-Time’s up. You said a second.

COOP
Andy, lemme ask you something.

ANDY
So you wanna talk for more than a
second, then.

COOP
Yeah.

ANDY
So what is it then? You think like
a couple of minutes?

COOP
At most, say, three minutes.

ANDY
Fine.

COOP
Do you really love Katie?

ANDY
Not really.

COOP
‘Cause I really really love her,
man! And if you have an ounce of
decency, you’ll do the right thing
and let her go.

ANDY
No way.

COOP
No way?

ANDY
Fuck no dude. She’s hot. In fact,
fuck off!

COOP
I will not fuck off! You’re making
a big mistake, buddy! That woman
isn’t your plaything!

ANDY
(calmly)
Coop, seriously, fuck off. I’m
sick of talking about this with
you.

COOP
Gawd!
EXT. ANDY’S BUNK - DAY

Coop, upset, kicks the door open and storms out, accidentally
getting his foot stuck in a BUCKET. He flails around trying
to get the bucket off his foot. He finally does, only to step
on a RAKE which hits him on the head. Dizzy from the hit to the
head, he steps on a BANANA PEEL, slips, and falls into a kiddy
pool. To cap it off, the bucket lands on his head.
EXT. PICNIC TABLE - DAY

The science kids, professor and Beth have built a huge
contraption out of popsicle sticks, a life preserver, a hula
hoop, six potato chip tins, etc.

BETH
Is this device gonna save my camp,
Henry?

PROFESSOR
I don’t know, but if it does,
we’ll all be heroes. In fact, I
just got word that if we pull his
off, I could be up for the Hopkins
Prize for Physics.

BETH
The Hopkins! What a dream! You’ll
get your tenure!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text: Coop sits on a rock by the lake crying. Gene approaches him.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
GENE
Be proud of who you are!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
COOP
Huh? Gene?

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
GENE
It’s not about the girl, Coop.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
COOP
It’s not?

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
GENE
It is, but if you can follow me
here, it...”isn’t”.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
COOP
Oh so it is and it isn’t.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
GENE
Yes! Now you are ready to be
taught “the new way”.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. ROCK BY THE LAKE - DAY
paragraphs.text:
COOP
Will you help with this...what was
it? “A new way”?
EXT. FIELD, ETC. - MONTAGE, DUSK
Coop’s TRAINING MONTAGE:
-- SLO-MO Coop and Gene begin a race through a field.

-- Gene tries teaching Coop a silly move in the dance shack.
The can of veggies watches. Coop can’t get it right.
-- Gene and Coop walking and talking in the forest.

-- Back in the dance shack: Gene, Coop, Can of Vegetables, and
some middle aged men and women in a group therapy session, all
wearing bathrobes. (Can of vegetables has a tiny bathrobe) Coop
is listening and nodding.

-- Gene and Coop continue their race through the field. Veggies
watches.

-- Gene and Coop continue their conversation in the woods. Coop
is nodding. He understands!
-- Coop, in the group therapy session, breaks into tears-
-- Coop, in the dance shack, finally NAILS the dance move!

-- Coop beats Gene in the race. Ge throws his hands in the air
and hugs Gene.
EXT. CAMP FIREWOOD SIGN - NIGHT

Victor, panting, with stubble, inexplicably caked in blood and
with a limp, runs up the path.

VICTOR
Abby! Abby! Oh my Abby! My dear
Abby!
EXT. BETH’S OFFICE - NIGHT

Neil rides the motorcycle up to the office. Obviously a stunt
man, he gets off the motorcycle, and passes behind a tree,
switching with the real Neil, who then approaches Beth, out of
breath.

BETH
What’s going on, Neil?

NEIL
Victor abandoned the canoe trip,
and now the kids are about to go
over Devil’s Canyon Rapids!
EXT. MOOSE RIVER - NIGHT

Neil and Victor’s campers are floating helplessly down the
Moose River, huddled together, cold and hungry. In the
foreground we see the perilous DEVIL’S CANYON RAPIDS.

CAMPERS
AHHHHHHHH!!!!!!!!!
INT. BETH’S OFFICE - NIGHT

BETH
Well where did he go?

NEIL
He wanted to come back to camp.

BEN
So you left the campers alone on
the river?

NEIL
Only Victor knows how to navigate
those rapids. We need to find him
and get him back to that river.
The phone RINGS.

Beth picks up the phone, and without even saying hello,
immediately yells...

BETH
It’s him!

A previously unseen Paul jumps into action trying to trace the
call on a series of phone devices.

BETH
Victor! We need to talk, bro!

PAUL
Keep him on as long as you can!

BETH
Victor? Victor? He’s not there.
Damn!

PAUL
I got him!

NEIL
He’s calling from inside the camp!

BETH
The only other phone is in the
infirmary! Let’s GO!!!
Beth and Neil bust out.
INT. INFIRMARY - SECONDS LATER - NIGHT

The NURSE tends to a sick girl. Beth and Neil bust in. They
break down the door and break everything in sight.

NURSE
Beth! What’s the matter?

BETH
Where’s the phone? WHERE’S THE
FUCKING PHONE!!?!?

NURSE
In the back room.

Beth and Neil go out of their way to break some more stuff and
go into the back room, where they find the phone off the hook,
and the window open.

BETH
Come on! We’re losing time!
They charge out of the infirmary.
EXT. REC HALL - NIGHT
People are filing into the rec hall for the talent show.
Victor runs onto the scene like a madman.

VICTOR
Abby! Abby I wanna make love to
you!
He finds Abby making out with Moose.

VICTOR
Abby?? Moose?

ABBY
Hey - uh - you’re gonna think I’m
so shallow, but what was your name
again?

VICTOR
Oh my god. Do you have any idea
what I’ve gone through to see you?

ABBY
You snooze, you lose, dude.
Beth and Neil arrive and seize Victor.

BETH
Victor Pulak - those kids are
about to go over Devil’s Canyon
Rapids.
EXT. MOOSE RIVER - NIGHT
The kids are inches away from going over the rapids.

CAMPERS
AHHHH!
EXT. REC HALL - NIGHT

A confused Victor looks over at Abby, then at Beth, then at
Neil.
A silent moment, then...

VICTOR
Let’s go!

Music kicks in as Neil and Victor run off. Beth waves, smiling
proudly.
EXT. CAMP FIREWOOD SIGN - NIGHT
Victor and Neil running full speed down the unpaved camp road.
EXT. PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT

Beth joins the Professor and his class as they put the final
touches on their Skylab tracking device.

PROFESSOR
Here we go.

He turns it on. It sputters and pops. A little paper printout
comes out of the bottom.

PROFESSOR
Oh, fuck my cock!

MEDIEVAL KID
What does it say?

PROFESSOR
According to the readout, Skylab
is going to land right on the Rec
Hall!

BETH
Oh no. When?

PROFESSOR
(reading more)
Sometime in the next 90 minutes!

CURE GIRL
Oh my god! That’s only one mix
tape!

MALLRAT GIRL
The talent show’s starting right
now!!

PROFESSOR
Beth, we need to evacuate the rec
hall immediately!
EXT. MOOSE RIVER - NIGHT

The raft full of campers is still inches from going over the
treacherous waterfall.

CAMPERS
Ahhhhhh!!!!
Victor and Neil arrive on the riverbank.

VICTOR
Stand back.

Victor runs toward the river and pulls out his buck-knife. We
stay on Neil as he watches and cheers Victor on.

NEIL
Wow - you’re a master! You’re
actually doing it! You - you just
saved them!
We see none of what he’s seeing.

Cut to Victor and the children triumphantly walking on the
shore. Neil walks up to Victor and shakes his hand,

NEIL (CONT’D)
Victor Pulak, you’re okay. You’re
okay by me. (Beat) I’m okay.
You’re okay.

VICTOR
And Neil, next time we go on a
camping trip, you drive the van!
They both laugh heartily and walk off arm in arm.
EXT. PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT

Professor and the class lay on the ground looking up at the
sky.

CURE GIRL
I wish there was another way.

MEDIEVAL KID
Maybe we should just let them die.

MORK GUY
No. My friend Jimmy’s in there.

CURE GIRL
You have a friend?

MORK GUY
I’m kidding.

CAPED BOY
What if we try to redirect the
trajectory of the object?

MALLRAT GIRL
That would be impossible.

Professor sits up and does some calculations in the air, then
sits back down.

PROFESSOR
Well there is... Nah.

MORK GUY
What?

PROFESSOR
There might be a way we could use
our device to slightly change its
direction. But, no. There’s no
way.

CAPED BOY
Why? What’s the problem?

PROFESSOR
Well...we’d have to somehow
repeatedly generate random numbers
between 1 and 20 in order to
calibrate the differential.

CURE GIRL
That would take some sort of
mainframe computer, and we for
sure don’t have one of those.

PROFESSOR
Then all is lost.

CAPED BOY
No it’s not.

CAPED BOY (CONT’D)
(now standing)
Any Dungeon Master worth his
weight in geldings goes nowhere
without his TWENTY SIDED DIE.
A huge symphonic CRESCENDO as they all slowly sit up and smile.
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT

All the campers and counselors are gathered for the big TALENT
SHOW.

Ben and Susie, on the steps, raise their hands to quiet
everyone down.

BEN
Good evening Camp Firewood!
I’d like to introduce your emcee
for the evening. We brought him
all the way from Kutscher’s
Country Club in the Catskill
Mountains --

BETH (O.S.)
Wait!
Beth busts in the door. Audience quiets down.

BETH
And my bad news is--

At this moment, Medieval Guy and Cure Girl bust into the rec
hall, TACKLE Beth to the ground, and whisper in her ear.

BETH
...and my bad news is, there is no
bad news. I was joking!

ALL
Beth! Good one! You rule!

BEN
Let’s give a Firewood welcome to
ALAN SHEMPER!

The audience breaks into hysterical Muppet-like screaming,
whooping and clapping.

ALAN SHEMPER, over 60, dressed in a powder yellow tuxedo takes
the stage.

SHEMPER
Hello, hello! Well, well, it’s
good t’be herein Camp Firewood.
You know, when I went to sleep
away camp -- in the STONE AGE --
but seriously it wasn’t the Stone
Age -- it was the ICE AGE -- No
really, it was the Stone Age.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: Beth, Medieval Guy and Cure Girl running back to the group.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT
EXT. PATH TO PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
SHEMPER (V.O.)
Actually, I went to Camp
Maschnudin in the Pine Hills of
Westchester County, New York. It
was very old fashioned - Camp
Machnudin...
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT

SHEMPER (CONT’D)
...so old we had to eat scrambled
Teradactyl eggs!

In the audience, Gail is laughing so hard that she’s moved to
tears.

SHEMPER (CONT’D)
But actually, Camp MASCH-NOODLE as
we liked to call it, was very fun,
very fun. Although back then, we
didn’t have cots - we had SLABS...
EXT. PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT

Beth, Medieval Guy and Cure Girl join the others who are
frantically packing up the device to carry it up to the rec
hall.

SHEMPER (V.O.)
...and instead of sleeping bags we
had -- SLEEPING PELTS -- ‘cause it
was so long ago.
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT

SHEMPER (CONT’D)
Our first act is a young man from
Bunk 2 who calls himself, Roger,
The Master Broom Balancer!

Out comes ROGER who performs the unimpressive feat of balancing
a broomstick on his hand.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
The group is setting up the device in a clearing directly
behind the rec hall.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Okay. Let’s pray to God this
works.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: They chat in Hebrew for a second: “Baruch, aaah, adonoi...”
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT
Three young girls, sit back to back on the stage, singing.

THREE GIRLS
Winter, Spring, Summer or Fall.
All you’ve got to do is call. And
I’ll be there, yes I will, ‘cause
you’ve got a friend.
(speaking)
That’s from us to bunk 7!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
Beth, Profesor and class are gathered around their device.
Caped Boy rolls the 20-sided die.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
CAPED BOY
18!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: Professor fiddles with the machine.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
Okay, we’re getting closer. Gimme
another.
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT

On stage, a fat Jewish kid, MARTY is doing an imitation of a
black comedian.

MARTY
White folks sound so stupid when
they get mad. They be like, “Hey
asshole! I’m going to kick your
behind!” But the brothers don’t
even have to raise their voice.
There motherfuckers be like,
“Don’t make me say it twice.”
The crowd eats it up. Moments later, Shemper on stage:

SHEMPER (CONT’D)
Be that as it may, this next act
is a young man named Bert
Flugalman, it says here that you
all know him as...

ALL
Mooooooooooose!

Moose comes out, lies down on the stage and lifts his legs over
his head. He holds a lighters to his ass and cuts a huge fart,
which creates a giant flash of fire, like a blowtorch. The
crowd cheers. He stands up and raises his arms in celebration.
IN THE AUDIENCE, Gail and Aaron are doubled over in laughter.

GAIL
That’s swwooo funny!

RON (O.S.)
Gail.

Out of nowhere, reveal Gail’s estranged husband RON, 40’s, with
a mustache, tweed coat, and wide wale corduroys.

GAIL
Ron...what are you doing here?

RON
Gail, I’ve been thinking about you
a lot. I want you back.

Gail looks over at Aaron and her entire group of supporting
campers.

GAIL
(Takes a deep breath)
Ronald I love you and I want to be
with you...(beat)...but...
The campers tense in excitement.

GAIL (CONT’D)
I’ve had too many woulda-coulda-
shoulda’s in my life and I’m just
sick and tired of being sick and
tired. So if you can’t deal with
that then to hell with you!

RON
You’ll come back to me Gail. I
know you will.
Gail looks over to Aaron, who nods in encouragement.

GAIL
(Defiantly)
I don’t think so Ronald Von
Kleinenshtein! I really don’t
think so!
Ron disappears. Her campers applaud. Gail and Aaron embrace.
BACK ON STAGE, Alan Shemper:

SHEMPER
And now, performing, “Day By Day”
from the musical Godspell, here’s
Susie’s drama group!
Susie comes out.

SUSIE
Before we start, I just wanted to
say, these campers you’re about to
see suck dick! Nevertheless,
please welcome them.

Susie walks off as as several young campers (led by Hopeful
Little Kid in the “Annie” sweatshirt) begin a performance of
“Day by Day” that is spectacularly outstanding by even Broadway
standards.

The drama group nails the end of their number, with the crowd
clapping along to the tune.

As soon as the song ends, the whole crowd collectively BOOS and
hisses them off the stage.
EXT. PICNIC TABLE - NIGHT
Caped Boy rolls the die.

CAPED BOY
5!

CURE GIRL
There’s not much time left!
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT

SHEMPER
You know, my favorite part of camp
was arts and crafts or as we used
to call it - ARTS AND FARTS AND
CRAFTS! But for real, I loved arts
arts and crafts. We would make
drawings -- CAVE DRAWINGS.
The whole audience is absolutely losing it, in hysterics.

SHEMPER (CONT’D)
One summer there were two
epidemics -- head lice and THE
PLAGUE! THE BUBONIC PLAGUE.
CUT TO:

The back door of the rec hall opens revealing the new Coop: He
is now wearing a half-shirt, tiny nylon shorts, tube socks
pulled up to his knees, and curiously permed hair. Everybody
notices him.
Coop finds Katie leaning against the opposite wall with Andy.

COOP
Katie, I came her to tell you I’m
leaving.

KATIE
What?

COOP
I’ve grown up a lot since before
dinner when we last talked.

KATIE
Where are you going to go?

COOP
I don’t know. See the world,
maybe. I’ve got two weeks before
school starts.

KATIE
But-

COOP
I want you to have this.

Coop gives her a small wrapped present - the size of a jewelry
box. He nods at her, then leaves.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: Still rolling the dice. Beth looks up to the sky.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Hurry up, you guys. I think I can
see it coming towards us.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT

paragraphs.text: INT. REC HALL - NIGHT
ON STAGE, Shemper:

SHEMPER
And finally tonight, ladies and
germs, as junior counselor who
goes by the name, “Steve.” Give
him a hand!
No applause - just coughing and murmur from the audience.

Steve comes out on stage, dressed in black. He raises his hands
in the air, waiting for everyone to quiet down.

GARY
Come on, do something!

JJ
Or get the fuck off the stage!
Beth rushes in to watch Steve’s act. They make eye contact.

Steve raises his hands higher in the air. He then THRUSTS his
hands forward. At that moment a small BREEZE wafts through the
rec hall.

ON KATIE: Katie opens Coop’s gift. From inside the small box
Katie pulls out Coop’s FLANNEL SHIRT. Her eyes well up with
tears.

KATIE
(under her breath)
Oh my God...
She brushes past Andy as she races for the door.
EXT. REC HALL - NIGHT

The building shakes at its foundations. Coop walks quickly away
from the rec hall with determination in his eyes. In the
background we see Katie run out of the rec hall towards him.

KATIE
COOP! COOP!

Coop can’t hear her. He keeps walking. Katie is sprinting to
catch up with him.

KATIE (CONT’D)
(crying)
Coop! Coop, don’t leave! Don’t
leave!
Coop keeps walking. He is at peace with his thoughts.

KATIE (CONT’D)
Coop! Oh Coop! I love you!
Coop stops and turns around just as Katie catches up to him.

KATIE
I didn’t know it for sure until
this second. But now I know it
like nothing I’ve ever known
before and it feels so good. I
love you, Coop! I love you!
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT

ON STEVE: Steve’s eyes are closed as he keeps his hands thrust
forward. The breeze steadily gathers strength, and starts
building to a fierce WIND blowing seemingly from Steve’s
fingers.

The wind gets so strong that it presses up against the faces of
all in the audience, blowing their hair back. Everything that’s
not bolted down flies to the back of the room. Benches are
tipped over.
Abby and Lindsay start making out.
EXT. REC HALL - NIGHT

COOP
How do I know this is real?

KATIE
Because, I’m telling you and
that’s the best I can do! I love
you Coop!

COOP
Say it again!

KATIE
I love you! I love you Gerald
Cooperberg!
The door of the rec hall flies off the hinges, just as...
Katie and Coop KISS!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
Professor turns one more screw and the machine starts to
vibrate.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
It’s working!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
They all look up to the sky. The object, now getting closer, is
hit by a HUGE GUST OF WIND and seems to change direction. They
watch it come down, down, until - CRASH! Onto the ground safely
away from the rec hall, falls a 40 foot long flaming piece of
metal with an American flag and the words “Skylab” painted on
it.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text: Beth runs over.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
BETH
Way to go guys!

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
Beth and Professor hug. She pats some blood off his face with a
wet towel.

paragraphs.scene: EXT. BEHIND THE REC HALL - NIGHT
paragraphs.text:
PROFESSOR
We did it!
INT. REC HALL - NIGHT

Finally Steve lowers his hands and the wind instantaneously
STOPS.
Silence for a long moment.

JJ puts two hands together and slowly starts to clap. She is
joined quickly by the rest of the camp. The clapping escalates
into a massive standing ovation. Everyone stands on the
benches, cheering, screaming, yelling, wetting their pants,
like a Beatles concert. Andy tries to put his arm around
Lindsey, but she pushes him away. Steve bows graciously.
EXT. REC HALL - NIGHT

The whole camp population exits the rec hall, walking back to
the bunks, many couples arm in arm. Focus on Coop and Katie in
full embrace.
DISSOLVE TO:
EXT. PARKING AREA - MORNING

Parents are arriving. Campers are saying their goodbyes,
loading the last of their bags onto the bus, taking pictures.

Beth stands by the entrance to the camp with some of the other
staff. Professor comes up to her.

PROFESSOR
Beth! I got it! The Hopkins Award!
And NASA’s hiring me to go work
for them at Cape Canaveral. I’ll
have a whole new life!

BETH
That’s great. I guess this means I
won’t see you again. I knew this
would happen. I knew I’d meet you
and fall in love and you’d dumb
me...

PROFESSOR
Wait, hold on Beth. I’ve already
told them that I need an apartment
for two people. And they’ll fly
you back here every summer!

BETH
(thrilled and happy)
Fuck you!!!

PROFESSOR
And I know we’ve had trouble with,
you know, but I was thinking when
the time comes we can adopt...

BETH
Shhh. It happened. I’m pregnant.
We’re going to have a baby.
They smile and hug. Gail and Aaron walk past them arm in arm.

GAIL
Goodbye, Beth.
Gail and Aaron smile at each other and kiss.

BETH
It looks like everything worked
out for you two.

AARON
Best thing’s ever happened in my
life.

GAIL
I hope you like shrimp cocktail,
because we want you to be the
guest of honor at our wedding
next week.

BETH
I’d be delighted. I just hope it’s
not jumbo shrimp - because I’m
allergic to oxymorons!
HUGE laughter from all.
EXT. PARKING AREA - MORNING
Coop and Gene catch each other’s eye. Coop hugs him.

NEARBY, Carol drives up in her car. Gene puts his bag in the
trunk and just before he gets in, turns back and makes eye
contact with the Can of Vegetables. He mouths “thank you”. The
Can of Veggies moves a bit in acknowledgement.
ELSEWHERE: Steve and Abby are making out.
EXT. REC HALL - MORNING

The professor joins his class as they lie down on the grass
where Skylab fell.

CURE GIRL
We saved everyone’s life and
they’ll never know it.

MALLRAT GIRL
I almost like it better that way.

CAPED BOY
It was us rolling the dice that
diverted it, right? Not that
enormous gust of wind, right?

PROFESSOR
Right.

MEDIEVAL KID
I feel like this entire summer,
which kind of sucked, has been
revitalized by the experience of
the last 24 hours.

MALLRAT GIRL
(sigh)
Yeah...it’s like...well, forget
it.

MEDIEVAL KID
You know what?

CAPED BOY
What?

MEDIEVAL KID
You guys are my best friends. In
fact you’re my only friends.

CAPED BOY
Yeah.

MORK GUY
Nanoo-nanoo.
EXT. BUNK AREA - MORNING
Katie comes out from the bunk area dragging her duffel bag.
Coop (now looking normal again) runs up to her.

COOP
Well this is it, babe!

KATIE
Yep...

COOP
So, obviously I think we should
try to meet in the city next
weekend. I’ll bring my parents and
you can talk to your Dad and Kim.

KATIE
Listen, Coop. Last night was fun.
And you were incredible heroic and
romantic. No doubt about it. And
that’s great. But I’ve thought
about it and my thing is this:
Andy is really hot. Don’t get me
wrong - you’re cute too, but
Andy’s just like, cut out of
marble. He’s gorgeous. He’s got an
incredible body and a beautiful
face...
87.

KATIE
He’s so hot that I genuinely don’t
care that he’s kind of lame -- I
don’t even care that he cheats on
me you know? I do like you more
than Andy, Coop, but I’m sixteen.
When I’m ready to get married, it
may be a different story, but
right now I’m so entirely about
sex, and I just want Andy. I wanna
grab his body and take him in. I
wanna fuck his brains out, you
know? So that’s where my
priorities are right now. Sex.
Specifically with Andy. And not
with you. But you are really nice.
Everyone thinks so. I’m sorry if
that’s not the direction you saw
things going with us. But I
totally want to be friends. You
better write me another letter,
okay? Cool.
A station wagon pulls up with Andy and his family.

ANDY
Katie, let’s go!

Katie gets in the car and it drives off. Coop stands alone
watching.

The camera cranes up to show the whole camp, cars and buses
pulling away, Coop standing alone, the last one left.

BETH
How ya doin?

COOP
Good.

Beth comes over to Coop and puts her arm around him. He smiles
at her and they walk off.

paragraphs.speaker: FADE TO BLACK
paragraphs.text: FADE TO BLACK
CREDITS ROLL...until...
CUT TO:
INT. BETH’S OFFICE - NIGHT
TITLE: “Ten Years Later - 9:30 AM”

In the lounge, all the characters have gathered. It is 1991.
They’re all much older now (27) -- they have gray hair and
beards and pot bellies. Beth and Professor are there with a
ten-year-old girl.

A white sheet is set-up against the wall and Gary is fiddling
with a giant VCR projector. JJ runs in.

JJ
Sorry I’m late. I thought we said
9:30.

SUSIE
No. We said 9. So we could start
at 9:30.
